description: Hot-swap, input protection, 15.0V UVLO, inrush control, and optional PMBus telemetry controller for the independent Makita 18V input
notes: Use with external Linear FETs; expose PMBus/fault test points even if no MCU is populated initially

description: 100V Linear FET for hot-swap, battery protection, and eFuse paths
notes: Back-to-back structure and parallel count depend on input fuse rating, SOA, and inrush current

description: Physical fuse near the Makita 18V battery input
notes: Start around 15A class and coordinate with XDP711 current limit, wiring, and connector rating

description: Mechanical and electrical interface for one Makita 18V battery
notes: Choose a mount with vibration retention and current rating suitable for 10A-class input

description: 55V four-switch buck-boost controller with optional I2C control for 19V/5A design output
notes: Evaluation module is close to 20V/5A; actual Mini PC is 19V/3.42A but validate with 5A dummy load for margin

description: 100V OptiMOS power MOSFET for 19V buck-boost power stage
notes: Thermal validation required at 95W output; parallel devices may be needed depending on layout and switching frequency
Current rating, saturation current, DCR, size, and temperature rise depend on switching frequency and ripple target

description: 20mOhm 12V/24V smart high-side switch with protection and diagnosis for Mini PC 19V output
notes: 5A continuous is thermally meaningful; verify copper area and derating

description: Cable-mount 5.5mm OD / 2.5mm ID DC plug for center-positive Mini PC 19V output
notes: Use center-positive wiring; board-side cable strain relief or a separate locking board connector is still a mechanical design item

description: Wide VIN non-synchronous boost/SEPIC/flyback controller with spread spectrum and hiccup for 24V/0.5A passive PoE output
notes: Use boost topology from protected input bus; hiccup is useful for cable fault conditions

description: 100V OptiMOS power MOSFET for 24V passive PoE boost stage
notes: Over-specified for 12W boost but useful for BOM commonality; smaller 80V/100V FET can replace later

description: Schottky or ultrafast diode for non-synchronous 24V boost stage
notes: Select voltage/current rating and recovery behavior after boost design calculation

description: 31mOhm 12V/24V smart high-side switch with protection and diagnosis for passive PoE 24V output
notes: Use together with a smaller fuse/PTC because the device current limit is higher than 0.5A PoE load current

description: Fuse or resettable PTC for passive PoE output cable protection
notes: Start around 0.75A to 1A class and validate with cable length, load startup, and short-circuit tests

description: Shielded 8P8C RJ45 jack without integrated magnetics for Data In and PoE Out
notes: Use one as Data In and one as PoE Out; passive PoE is fixed to 100Mbps operation in this pinout

description: Quad low-capacitance ESD protection diode for RJ45 data pins 1,2,3,6
notes: Protect only the data pins; PoE 24V pins need power TVS/fuse treatment

description: Input TVS, 19V output TVS, 24V output TVS, EMI filters, and shield RC/DNP options
notes: Values depend on cable length, enclosure, and rover shield/GND policy

description: Bulk capacitors, ceramic capacitors, sense resistors, feedback dividers, compensation parts, gate resistors, and pull resistors
notes: Values depend on final LM51772 and LM51561 design calculations and layout

description: LEDs and test points for battery input, 19V, 24V, fault, PMBus/fault pins, and output sense
notes: Keep enough test access for standalone board bring-up without MCU
